topic: Case study: transaction data -> feature matrix
scope: Concrete walk from raw rows to a usable matrix applying defined principles.
key_concepts: Show how raw transaction logs (User X bought Item Y on Date Z) are not yet ready for a machine learning model.
Demonstrate defining the unit (User) and the target (Will churn next month).
Construct the features by aggregating the transaction history up to the index time (e.g., total spent last 30 days, count of unique categories bought).
The output is a flat, engineered tabular feature matrix ready for training.
examples: Input: Transaction table with columns (Transaction ID, User ID, Timestamp, Amount, Category).
Output: Feature matrix with columns (User ID, Target Churn, Count_Transactions_30d, Sum_Amount_30d, Ratio_Electronics_30d).
exclusions: Do not provide code snippets. Explain the process in narrative form.

figures.caption: Transformasi dari log transaksi (data mentah) ke matriks fitur
figures.description: Diagram showing raw event rows fanning inward and aggregating into a single summarized row per user ID.
